reason: Check A0 scope, non-goals, source authority, and acceptance criteria.
Check current architecture audit distinguishes accepted contracts from working-tree candidates.
Check reference extraction preserves source roles: pi-mono for minimal layering, Claude Code for mature module/function design, and current SetupAgent as project truth.
Check persisted grill-me decisions are carried into target architecture and do not rely on chat memory.
Check target HLD is grounded in explicit current-problem/reference-design/project-landing mappings.
Check that the new task preserves the handoff constraints and does not regress to narrow bugfix framing.
Check target architecture against existing setup loop semantics contract.
Check repair-loop planning against existing schema repair contract.
Check capability/tool-scope planning against existing stage-aware allowlist contract.
Check context-pipeline planning against existing context assembly contract.
Check state/context planning against existing governance persistence boundaries.
Check outer-harness boundary is preserved.
Check SkillPack remains prompt-layer only.
Check truth-write and provider validation assumptions are not silently overridden.
Check target HLD consistency against PRD, audit, reference lessons, grill decisions, and accepted setup contracts.
Check ownership contracts and invariants are consistent with HLD, PRD, and active setup specs.
Check verification plan covers old bug class, capability drift, typed SSE, and provider attribution.
Check rollout slices preserve scope boundaries, forbidden scope, and check cadence.
Check open questions and recommended defaults match confirmed user decisions and do not block A1.
Check A1 implementation against explicit loop/output/repair/event/persistence scope, forbidden scope, and finish-reason taxonomy rules.
